recent_linkedin_signals.excerpt: Forvis Mazars en France recruits at scale across audit and consulting, reviewing around 200,000 applications and hiring 5,000 people every year. They wanted an efficient way to identify skills early in the process, reduce reliance on CV screening, and deliver a stronger candidate experience. They partnered with Maki to implement science-backed skills assessments, with immersive experiences designed to engage new generations of talent. Results: Forvis Mazars reduced the duration of its selection process by 60%, while achieving a 96% assessment completion rate. Hear directly from the ex-HR Director of Forvis Mazars France in this new case study: https://lnkd.in/ezKuXRuY

sources.name: MakiMaki | LinkedIn
sources.content:
MakiMaki is a premium fast-casual sushi bar that focuses exclusively on sushi rolls. As a specialty Japanese sushi roll shop, we offer two types of rolls, traditional 8-piece cutup maki and cone-shaped temaki or hand rolls.
MakiMaki brings sushi rolls into the fast-casual dining category by preparing all rolls in an assembly line right in front of customers’ eyes, while also allowing for customization with additional ingredients and sauces. We have perfected the process of preparing fresh, custom-made sushi rolls of 5-star quality and proudly serve our products at reachable prices.
MakiMaki pioneered this unique and engaging approach to enjoying fresh, tailor-made sushi that combines “in-and-out” pace with high quality and authenticity and our ultimate mission is to bring this experience to every New Yorker.

sources.name: Makisu | LinkedIn
sources.content:
Makisu | 511 followers on LinkedIn. The Sunny Side of Sushi | At Makisu, we have a healthy obsession with sushi… We literally travelled across the globe to find the most flavorful & awesomest recipes to put the sunshine back into your sushi-experience (it’s a hard job, but somebody’s got to do it…)
Makisu | 511 followers on LinkedIn. The Sunny Side of Sushi | At Makisu, we have a healthy obsession with sushi… We literally travelled across the globe to find the most flavorful & awesomest recipes to put the sunshine back into your sushi-experience (it’s a hard job, but somebody’s got to do it…)
Choose one of our signature sushi rolls or compose a maki as unique as you are using our 4-step “create-your-own-maki process”. With more than 30 fresh and delicious ingredients to choose from, there are over 3 million possibilities to brighten up your taste buds…Only one question remains : “How do you roll?”
Makisu, is the leading fast casual sushi franchise in Belgium. 🍣 In 2023, we were tasked with rebranding Makisu. The goal? Helping them to get back internal clarity after a decade of dedication towards processes and products. We worked with the team to help them rekindle the flame, voice their ambition and refresh their identity for the next decades.
All qualities we had to distill into a renewed brand vision ─while tying back to their slogan “the sunny side of sushi”─ that was then unequivocal: “To feed our good nature”. If Makisu was a “good-natured eatery” for many guests it was mostly because, behind the scenes, Makisu is a “good-natured company” to its employees.

Dans un marché en pleine transformation, comment concilier volume, exigence et expérience candidat ? Comment structurer une approche de recrutement à la fois cohérente, performante et scalable ?

Ce mercredi 20 mai à 16h, Mélanie Gobin-Roux, Directrice Recrutement et Attractivité chez Deloitte, répondra à ces questions aux côtés de Paul Louis Caylar, co-fondateur et Directeur des Opérations chez Maki People.

Si vous recrutez dans l'audit et le conseil ou si vous repensez votre approche de recrutement, vous ressortirez avec des retours concrets, directement applicables.

sources.name: MakiPeople LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-12T14:39:05.538Z
sources.content:
Forvis Mazars en France recruits at scale across audit and consulting, reviewing around 200,000 applications and hiring 5,000 people every year.

They wanted an efficient way to identify skills early in the process, reduce reliance on CV screening, and deliver a stronger candidate experience.

They partnered with Maki to implement science-backed skills assessments, with immersive experiences designed to engage new generations of talent.

Results: Forvis Mazars reduced the duration of its selection process by 60%, while achieving a 96% assessment completion rate.

Hear directly from the ex-HR Director of Forvis Mazars France in this new case study: https://lnkd.in/ezKuXRuY
---

sources.name: Maxime Legardez Coquin LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-07T16:42:00.204Z
sources.content:
big FAIL today 🍰 🍰 🍰 
two of my BDRs spent the day walking Manhattan in suits, carrying handmade Maki chocolate cakes 
the plan was simple. show up at Fortune 500 HQ and hand a cake to the CHRO.
the result? rejected at every single door. security said no, every time.
and honestly, I am so proud of them.
they tried something weird. they failed in style. they kept their sense of humor through the whole thing.
this is what building a real company looks like. you ship the strange ideas. you let your team take big swings. you celebrate the attempt, even when the bat misses.
now we have a problem though. a lot of cake.
so if you are a CHRO or HR leader in Manhattan and want to share a goûter with your team this afternoon, DM me. just tell your security guard the cake is on its way. the guys will come back, and this time with backup.
imagine what this team will pull off once we actually partner together.
who is in?
well done Lenny Brenner & Rob Heppenstall
---

sources.name: Maxime Legardez Coquin LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-06T17:49:39.083Z
sources.content:
9 months. one signature. take off.
today Captain Natasha Brennan closed a partnership with one of the most iconic Fortune 500 brands in the world. ✈️ ✈️✈️
people underestimate what enterprise deals really take. months of conversations. on site meetings. proof points. trust built one meeting at a time.
these don't happen on a demo call. they happen when your team shows up again, and again, and again.
what i love about Tash: she never lost altitude. calm in turbulence. 
today we finally lift off together.
day one of a long flight. we're just climbing.
to every founder pushing through long enterprise cycles: keep going. the signature is just the boarding pass. the real work starts now. 👏

sources.name: Maxime Legardez Coquin LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-06T12:28:59.317Z
sources.content:
only one vendor in the room. that was us 👏 
last week Aiden Loe and Jinji SHEN flew to New Orleans for SIOP 2026, THE conference that brings together the sharpest minds in I/O psychology.
everyone else in the room was deep inside Fortune 500 orgs. Walmart, J&J, Deloitte, Microsoft, Ford, PepsiCo, Citi etc.
the mood has shifted. last year SIOP was sceptical about AI in hiring. this year it wasn't. the conversation moved from "can AI do this" to "how do we do it responsibly."
transparency. evaluation frameworks. traceability. fairness.
the EU AI Act is pushing shared accountability between providers and deployers. technical documentation is no longer optional.

Aiden and Jinji also took the stage with their research: "from experts to LLMs, validating open ended scoring in high volume recruitment."
at Maki we believe that's the only way to build something truly transformational when AI meets hiring. rigour and science at every layer.

want Aiden's full debrief from the conference? drop Aiden Loe 🤙  in the comments and he'll send his complete notes your way !!
---

sources.name: Aiden Loe LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-06T09:55:40.283Z
sources.content:
Last week, Jinji SHEN and I attended SIOP 2026. Always a pleasure to reconnect with old friends and meet new ones. The kind of conversations that happen in the hallways are just as valuable as the sessions themselves.

A few things stood out.
The mood has shifted. SIOP 2025 was sceptical about AI. SIOP 2026 was not. The question is no longer whether AI belongs in recruitment and selection; it's how to do it responsibly.

Responsible AI dominated the conversation. Transparency, evaluation frameworks, traceability, and fairness were everywhere. The EU AI Act is pushing towards shared accountability between providers and deployers - which means technical documentation is no longer optional. 

The infrastructure is being built. LLMs are now being used for job analysis, competency modelling, SJT development, constructed response scoring, interview optimisation, and recruiter debriefs. What impressed us wasn't the use cases themselves — it was the rigour behind them. Theoretical frameworks, iterative testing, and systematic evaluation. This is science-backed automation at scale. That's what we do at MakiPeople.

But some important conversations are still missing, and I'll admit I'm biased towards using LLMs in recruitment and selection here. Score injection, adversarial test-taking, and synthetic data testing barely featured. And perhaps the biggest open question of all: does any of this actually improve the predictive validity of relevant outcomes above and beyond traditional assessments? The honest answer is that the evidence is still lacking. For a field moving this fast, that gap is one we cannot afford to ignore.

There is still a lot to build, evaluate and prove. And I am excited by the thought of it! Looking forward to the next SIOP 2027!

*We are still looking for an AI Scientist to join the team. If you know someone who could be a great fit, please send them my way. See link in comments. =)
---

sources.content:
A quiz can tell you if someone knows facts about AI. It cannot tell you if they can use it. That is why Maki uses three methods, not one.

Situational judgment tests put candidates in realistic AI work scenarios so you see how they decide, not how they describe what they might do.

Structured behavioral grids capture how they have actually applied AI in practice, structured and comparable across candidates.

Conversational cognitive tasks, coming in Mochi 3.5, will show you how someone uses AI to work through genuinely complex problems.

Three methods. Four dimensions. One reliable signal. See exactly how it works https://lnkd.in/eDCU_jmE

sources.name: MakiPeople LinkedIn Post - 2026-04-14T12:00:21.376Z
sources.content:
"Proficient with AI tools."

Half your candidates have that line. None of them mean the same thing by it.
Most AI assessments on the market are a tool checklist in disguise. Can you name a large language model? Congratulations, you passed. That’s trivia, not measurement.

Maki just launched AI proficiency assessment. It focuses on four dimensions that drive AI performance at work: how fast someone adapts to new AI tools, how well they collaborate with AI to produce real outputs, whether they apply ethical judgment in AI-assisted decisions, and whether they understand enough about how AI works to catch when it is wrong.

Built by Maki's dedicated science team. Grounded in workplace research. If you’re hiring people who will use AI daily, read this before your next intake. https://lnkd.in/eDCU_jmE

sources.name: Benjamin Chino LinkedIn Post - 2026-04-10T12:23:07.783Z
sources.content:
If you’re using AI in hiring, you’re probably already non-compliant with the EU AI Act. We just turned 12 months of work with Fortune 2000 legal teams into a free 20-page playbook.

The issue isn’t the deadline. It’s that most teams don’t understand where their responsibility starts; and where their vendors’ responsibility ends. If you’re using AI for screening, assessments, or interviews, you’re likely in “high-risk” territory. And if you process candidates based in the EU, your HQ doesn’t matter. You’re in scope.

Over the past year, here’s what keeps coming up in legal reviews:
- Vendors who can’t clearly explain their risk classification
- Missing documentation on training data, bias mitigation, or auditability
- No clear answer on who is liable if something goes wrong

That’s where most teams are exposed.

So Aiden Loe, Moric J. and I built a playbook to make this concrete. Inside, you’ll find:
- The exact questions legal teams expect you to ask your vendors
- What you’re accountable for as a deployer; not the vendor
- A step-by-step plan to start now; not in Q3 2026

No fluff. Just what’s actually being asked in enterprise compliance reviews. Comment “AI Act” and I’ll send it to you.
---

sources.content:
# MakiPeople
Scale hiring. Perfectly.
**Website:** https://www.makipeople.com/ | **Employees:** 121 | **Followers:** 11,551
**HQ:** New York City, New York, US | **Founded:** 2021 | **Industries:** Software Development
**Hashtags:** #hrtech, #talents, #recrutement

## About
For decades, enterprise hiring ran on the ATS, with an accumulation of software built around it to patch every gap. None of it was built to think or act. Every decision still required a human. The bottleneck was never the data. It was the absence of intelligence running on top of it.

Maki is that intelligence. The lovable layer on top of the unloved software. AI agents running hiring end to end, from first application to hire decision, on top of your existing ATS.

Organizations like H&M, PwC, Amazon, Nestlé, and Sephora already run Maki at scale. Our global partnership with H&M saved 250,000 recruiter hours, cut time-to-hire by 4x, and reduced turnover by 22%, delivering $85M ROI.

And for staffing firms and RPOs, where hiring is the entire business? This is not a feature addition. It is a business model transformation. The firms moving first with Maki are delivering a fundamentally different service: lower cost, higher quality, at a scale no manual process can match. We're already deep in it with some of the largest staffing players globally.

90% automation. 3x faster time-to-hire. 98% candidate NPS. Every hire makes the system smarter.

Learn more: makipeople.com

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-28T15:49:40.922Z
sources.content:
Last night 5 senior HR leaders agreed.

In 2026, the question is no longer whether to use AI, but how to generate RoI from it.

Bolt. S&P Global. Wärtsilä. Cognizant. GSK.

That's the moment the category moves.

Twelve months ago this would have been an education session. Is AI in hiring even real?

Last night it was a impact conversation. Which lever, how quickly, which monitoring, which workflow.

 A few patterns from the room:

→ The CV is dying. Candidates are using AI to generate hundreds of tailored CVs per role. The signal is gone. Skills, scored consistently against a clear taxonomy, is the only defendable replacement.

→ End-to-end automation pilots are emerging across enterprise hiring. Grades route candidates into assessments. Scores route them into tiered interviews. No recruiter in the funnel until the hiring manager stage. Multiple leaders at the table said that's where they want to be.

→ "AI proficiency" is the new screening question for early careers. Every leader wants to assess it. Nobody has a defensible way to. Maki does.

At the table:
Victoria Clarke
Andrew Sinclair
Scott Haber
Adrian Dobson
Lucy Hegarty

and of course my amazing cohost Marc de Wind

If you're a senior HR leader, what's the line that's landed in your room this year?

#TalentAcquisition #HRLeadership #FutureOfWork #AIinHR #Hiring
---

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-19T09:09:53.877Z
sources.content:
What happens when a candidate drops out of an AI interview halfway through — then comes back days later?

Most systems start over. Zero context.

Not ours.

Benjamin Chino and the product team shipped memory for our AI interviewer. 

When a candidate returns, the system picks up exactly where they left off — context, tone, progress, all of it.

At enterprise scale, candidate drop-off is the biggest silent cost in hiring. If even 5% of candidates who abandon return and complete the process, that's thousands of recovered hires across a large deployment.

But they won't come back if coming back means starting from scratch.

This is the kind of feature that never makes a pitch deck but separates a demo from a production system.
---

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-05-12T09:38:38.233Z
sources.content:
Data sovereignty isn't a feature. It's a prerequisite.

Maki now offers full US data residency alongside EU hosting. 

Global teams can choose where their data lives.

This sounds technical. It's actually strategic.

Every enterprise procurement conversation in the last 6 months — whether in New York, London, or Paris — reaches the same question: "Where does our candidate data sit?"

If you can't answer that clearly, the deal stalls. Doesn't matter how good your AI is.

Building true data residency isn't about setting up a new server. 

It's about ensuring every component — processing, storage, computing, audit trails — respects the same boundary. 

One leak and the whole promise breaks.

This is the infrastructure work that makes or breaks enterprise AI adoption.
---

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-04-02T07:13:37.833Z
sources.content:
What happens when AI generates a hiring signal you disagree with?

Under the EU AI Act, you MUST be able to step in. 
Override. 
Explain why.

This isn't a theoretical question for us at Maki. It's a design principle we've followed since day one.

Our AI agents recommend scores grounded in psychometric science. But the recruiter always has the final word. 

Always. 

That's not a compromise — it's what makes the system trustworthy at scale.

In practice, this means: 
→ Every AI-generated score is based on objective measures and comes with an explanation 
→ Recruiters can override any recommendation with a documented rationale 
→ Audit trails are built into every decision point

Organisational transformations that succeed are the ones with the best technology. They are the ones that built trust between the system and the people using it.

AI in hiring is no different.
---

This playbook on how to evaluate voice AI Interview agents is a must-read.

And it may be the difference between at-scale transformation and a legal liability or employer brand damage.

So do not miss out on this one, go to the post below from my fellow co-founder Maxime Legardez Coquin, comment "PLAYBOOK" and you will not only thank yourself but get a stylish hat on top !
---

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-03-26T08:37:32.729Z
sources.content:
Google cloud recently published a case study about Maki. And it's always special when it comes from Google.

For a team our size competing against established players with thousands of engineers, this is a proud milestone.

What Google highlighted: 
→ Real-time AI-powered interviews at scale 
→ Psychometric scoring grounded in science 
→ Infrastructure that processed 1M+ candidate applications in under 5 days

Behind those numbers is an operations story I rarely see told.

Processing a million applications isn't just a compute problem. It's a reliability problem, a fairness problem, and a trust problem. 

Every single one of those applications represents a person hoping for a fair shot. 

If the system goes down, slows down, or produces inconsistent results — that's not a bug. It's a broken promise.

That's why we built Maki on reliability, fairness and trust from day one.

Huge credit to Benjamin Chino and the MakiPeople team for the technical and product work that made this possible, and to the Google Cloud (cc Mathilde Baldy) team for sharing our story.

For teams exploring AI in hiring; Maki is now available directly through the Google Cloud Marketplace.

→ Case study link in comments
---

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-03-24T15:07:06.110Z
sources.content:
There's a pivotal moment in every enterprise AI deployment when leadership inquires, "What’s the actual impact?"

At Maki, we now have the answer from a significant enterprise deployment:

- 40% reduction in time-to-hire, globally
- 158,000+ manager hours freed up, equating to over €2.5M in time value
- 16,000 hires processed through the new operating model

When considering quality of hire, retention improvements, and speed at scale, the total impact reaches double-digit millions.

This is not a pilot or a proof of concept; this is the reality of production AI when it is effectively operationalized and deployed at scale. 

And it's only the beginning.
---

I had a tremendous Wednesday evening this week.

Maki People hosted a private dinner for senior HR and TA leaders in Amsterdam.

Around the table: leaders from Booking.com, Avolta, McKinsey & Company, Rabobank, MUFG, ABB, and Remote.

What I appreciate most about these evenings is the quality of the conversation. No agenda to push, no stage to perform on. Just people navigating a very similar set of challenges, in very different contexts, talking openly about them.

Thank you to everyone who joined. Looking very much forward to the next one !

sources.name: Paul LinkedIn Post - 2026-02-04T17:01:24.543Z
sources.content:
This is what “real AI adoption” looks like. And MakiPeople is part of it.

Thank you Early Builders & Headline for mapping the AI tools that actually stick. of course, OpenAI, and Cursor are everywhere. transformational. universal. but what i find super interesting is the bigger shift underneath: we’re moving from chat… to agentic workflows. with structure. grounding. context. where data creates a new type of efficiency and intelligence.

I’m proud to see Maki included alongside teams like Lovable, Mistral AI, and Granola. It’s a strong signal that AI in hiring is shifting from experimentation to infrastructure.

And we’re just getting started. thank you Headline for the work & perspective !
---

Makani | 26,917 followers on LinkedIn. Harnessing wind energy with kites to create renewable electricity | Makani aimed to enable more people around the world to have access to clean, affordable wind power by developing energy kites, an airborne wind energy technology that used a wing tethered to a ground station, to efficiently harness energy from the wind.
By replacing the massive steel towers of conventional wind turbines with lightweight hardware and smart software, Makani hoped to unlock access to wind resources too expensive or impractical to access with traditional wind technologies. In 2020, Makanis journey as a company came to an end.
Learn more about the team’s 13 year journey building an entirely new type of wind energy technology by visiting: www.x.company/projects/makani
